sn2.22:1.1: Standing to one side, Khema the deva’s son recited these verses in the Blessed One’s presence.
sn2.22:3.1: It’s not good, the doing of the deed
sn2.22:3.2: that, once it’s done, you regret,
sn2.22:4.1: It’s good, the doing of the deed
sn2.22:4.2: that, once it’s done, you don’t regret,
sn2.22:5.1: You should do prudently, right away,
sn2.22:5.2: what you know is for your own benefit.
sn2.22:5.3: A thinker, enlightened, you shouldn’t strive
sn2.22:5.4: for the sake of the cart driver’s thinking.
sn2.22:6.1: Just as a cart driver who—having left
sn2.22:6.3: and climbed onto an uneven path—
sn2.22:7.1: in the same way, a fool—having departed from Dhamma
sn2.22:7.3: having fallen into the mouth of death—
